# Questions in Livestream 250

Live Stream #250: The Origin Of All Live Streams

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=568" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:28</a> | In Genesis 48:22, when did Jacob actually take anything from the Amorites? | He argued Jacob was not inventing the claim out of nowhere, but read it against the broader patriarchal setting rather than as a transparent self-justification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=728" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:08</a> | Is Jacob lying again in Genesis 31:6? | He said Jacob is not necessarily lying there; the passage needs to be read in context rather than assuming deception every time Jacob speaks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:16</a> | In Genesis 31:10-13, does Jacob embellish what God previously told him in 31:3? | He allowed some compression or framing for Rachel and Leah, but did not treat it as a fabricated divine speech. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1310" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:50</a> | Do we know where the wilderness of Shur, Marah, Elim, and the wilderness of Zin were? | Some locations are known more securely than others. He treated Shur as identifiable, while several later stops remain uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:16</a> | In Judges 9:45, is the salting of the city literal or idiomatic? | Literal. He read it as a real act of devastation and symbolic ruin, not just a figure of speech. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1842" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:42</a> | Would salting someone's yard get someone charged with vandalism? | Yes. He said salting another person's yard would destroy property and could be charged as vandalism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1928" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:08</a> | Was the salt in the example table salt or some other kind of salt? | It was table salt, but he said any salt can damage crops by dehydrating plant roots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=1948" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:28</a> | Is the long salting of Mesopotamia irreversible? | No. He said salting is not irreversible, though it can take a long time for salt to leach out. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=2087" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:47</a> | Where does the quoted line in James 4:5 come from? | He did not think it was a clean quotation of one verse, but more likely a summary or thematic reuse of Old Testament ideas, especially from the Greek tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=2380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:40</a> | Were cupbearers typically eunuchs, and were they drawn from lower classes or slavery? | He treated court cupbearers as palace servants who could indeed be eunuchs, but not simply as a generic low-class office. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=3040" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:40</a> | What extravagant dish would you love to make or try? | He mentioned Japanese delicacies such as fugu and herring roe, while saying he could not think of many specific dishes offhand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=3329" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:29</a> | Do you dislike the term `dead language`? | No. He regarded it as a normal and acceptable label for languages no longer spoken colloquially. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=3732" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:12</a> | Why did God create dreams? | He answered in strongly physiological terms, linking dreams to sleep, brain function, and bodily reset rather than treating every dream as mystical by default. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=3830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:50</a> | How does one record dreams? | Write them down quickly. He said there is only a short window after waking to intentionally remember a dream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:14</a> | Can dreams have a spiritual element? | Yes. He said dreams can have a spiritual element or be influenced by spirits, though not every dream should be treated that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:50</a> | How can you prevent certain dreams or stop remembering them? | He said some can be reduced by mental discipline and by not dwelling on them afterward, unless the dream is tied to a deeper ongoing issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4416" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:36</a> | What is the best vampire movie you can recommend? | He picked *Let the Right One In* and also noted *What We Do in the Shadows* as a good comedy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4533" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:33</a> | In Nehemiah 2:13, what were the walls made of? | Stone. He said Jerusalem's fortifications were rebuilt from local stone after earlier destruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:00</a> | In John 21, did the apostles go back to fishing after they knew Jesus had risen? | Yes. He said John 21 comes after the resurrection appearances in John 20. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=4684" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:04</a> | Would the apostles have been expected to continue ministry, or to go back to work? | He said it was culturally normal for students of a teacher to return to their villages and trades after their period of learning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=5058" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:18</a> | In John 21, what is the point of shifting from `agape` to `phileo` if they are synonymous? | He said the slight wording change emphasizes the point and gets around Peter's failure to grasp the repeated question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=5298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:18</a> | What is the best approach for understanding the tabernacle: Christological readings or cultural context? | He preferred beginning with the tabernacle's own cultural and symbolic context, then moving to Christological application afterward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=5913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:33</a> | Did anyone from the Daily Wire reach out to interview you about your books? | No. He said no one from the Daily Wire or similar major platforms had reached out to him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=5970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:30</a> | Do you know anyone on YouTube who does what you do but specializes in ancient Persia? | No. He said he knows scholars of ancient Persia, but not an online channel doing the same kind of work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=6317" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:17</a> | Did ancient people expect to die by age forty because average life expectancy was low? | No. He said the low averages are driven largely by infant and child mortality, not by adults universally expecting early death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=6709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:49</a> | How were charioteers promoted in Egypt? | He described the role as already relatively elevated, with promotion potential through military hierarchy and demonstrated competence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=7148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:08</a> | Do the Elephantine papyri show that the Hebrew Bible was written very late? | No. He argued the papyri do not support that skeptical conclusion and instead reflect a mixed, Aramaic-speaking Jewish setting with its own irregularities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=7860" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:00</a> | Does 1 Corinthians 6:19 imply each individual Christian is a temple of the Spirit? | Yes. He rejected the claim that God can only inhabit one temple in a way that would rule out individual indwelling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=10115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:35</a> | What do you think of Yonatan Adler's thesis that almost no one practiced Judaism until the Hasmonean period? | He said it depends on definitions: if `Judaism` means post-temple Judaism there is a point, but Israelite religion clearly predates that period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=10271" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:11</a> | Is Ephesians 5:22-23 egalitarian or complementarian, and does headship imply a boss-employee relationship? | He resisted imposing either modern label too simply and said the passage does not make the husband a boss over an employee. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=10572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:12</a> | In Ezekiel 16:3, who is the Hittite mother? | He connected the language to patriarchal origins, saying Amorite men intermarried with Hittite local women in the Haran/Syrian setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=10787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:47</a> | Do books we write count as worship to God or simply as honoring God? | Either can be true. He said writing can be worship, can honor God, or can do both depending on the writer's intention. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=10858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:58</a> | What was the size of the wall Nehemiah repaired? | He said it was the fortification around Jerusalem, but he was not sure what exact measurements can be recovered. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=11003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:23</a> | What is your current view on the large numbers in Exodus and Numbers? | He still held to a clan view, distinguishing clans, clades, and family units while noting that many interpretations exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=11186" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:26</a> | What did the snake symbolize in ancient Egypt, and how does that affect Exodus 7:8-13? | He said the cobra carried major royal and sacred symbolism in Egypt, which sharpens the force of the staff-to-serpent sign in Pharaoh's court. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=11670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:30</a> | What are your thoughts on binding and loosing in Matthew 16:19? | He read it primarily as opening or closing access through the gospel, with church authority involved but not arbitrary power. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=12132" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:12</a> | What was the historical and cultural background of the tabernacle? | He connected it to Egyptian religious tents and shrine furniture that marked sacred space, not merely to military tents. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=12357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:57</a> | Do you think Isaiah wrote all of Isaiah, or do you believe in Second and Third Isaiah? | He favored single authorship or unity of Isaiah and rejected multiplying separate Isaiahs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=12458" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:38</a> | Does the singular Isaiah of the Dead Sea Scrolls support the unity of Isaiah? | Yes. He said the Great Isaiah Scroll preserves the same structural inflection point used to argue for unity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=12579" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:39</a> | How do James 4:11-12 and 1 Corinthians 5 fit together regarding separation from unrepentant believers? | He said they address different situations: James warns against sinful condemnation, while Paul deals with open, unrepentant scandal inside the church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=12970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:10</a> | Have you watched *House of David*, and does its portrayal of kingship fit the ancient Near Eastern context? | He had not watched it, but said a European-style portrayal of kingship and untouchable rulers would not fit the ancient Near East well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=13056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:36</a> | Why does Genesis 14 call Tidal `king of nations`? | He said `nations` may not be the best translation there and likely refers to a non-Israelite city-state or region. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=13242" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:42</a> | If building a temple outside Jerusalem was taboo, why did God not say anything until Josiah? | He said ritual law is prescriptive: the sacred site is defined by what God commands, not by an exhaustive list of forbidden alternatives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=13820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:20</a> | What are the most compelling young-earth arguments? | He said polonium halos were probably the most compelling to him, though he also said that argument has been heavily critiqued. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=14095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:55</a> | Is Noah's age in Genesis 7:6 a clan age? | Yes. He treated Noah's 600 years as a clan age rather than an ordinary individual lifespan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=14197" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:37</a> | Why was Abraham shocked at having a son in old age if the patriarchal ages were literal? | He said the shock supports reading the long patriarchal ages as clan ages rather than normal personal lifespans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=14298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:18</a> | Why are the Cain and Seth lines structurally parallel? | He said the parallel structure is narrative design, using the genealogies as a teaching device that shows both lines converging toward the flood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=14436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:36</a> | What are your thoughts on Michael Heiser's view that demons are disembodied Nephilim? | He rejected it, saying Scripture presents the Nephilim as men and does not link demons to disembodied Nephilim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=15833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:23:53</a> | Is there a connection between the Gilgamesh plant and the Genesis tree of life? | He said a connection is possible, but tree-of-life motifs were widespread, including in Egypt, so it is not unique to Gilgamesh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/stST4hqw2F4?t=15942" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:42</a> | Do you concur with Augustine's just war theory and its application to current violence involving Iran? | He declined to answer because just war theory is outside his specialization and he did not remember the details well enough. |
